plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero to eliminate high risk.
Trim concentrated positions like SPY and SMH to reduce single name and sector risk.
Add to the income ETF QQQI to build a more stable income component.
Buy GLD for diversification and as a potential hedge against market downturns.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a significant cash buffer.
Expect lower volatility and a smaller target return.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation and low drawdowns. Expect a target monthly return around 1.5%. Significant risk reduction is achieved by cutting leverage and diversifying away from tech concentration.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL) and sell others (SPAL) to manage risk.
Reduce concentration in SPY and SMH to meet mandate limits.
Add to the strong performer CRWD to capitalize on its momentum.
Buy ANET, a high-scoring name, to diversify into a quality setup.
This plan balances growth with risk control, maintaining some tech exposure.
Expect a target monthly return around 2.5% with moderate drawdowns.
balanced.read: This balanced plan seeks risk-first growth, targeting around 2.5% monthly. It trims leverage and concentration while adding to strong performers and a new quality name. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Trim SPY and QQQI to free up capital for growth areas.
Increase exposure to semiconductors (SMH) and AI/Software (CRWD).
Add to the leveraged semiconductor ETF SOXL and buy the 3x tech ETF TECL.
Buy ARM, a high-scoring name, to further concentrate in momentum.
This plan aggressively pursues growth by leveraging strong setups and tech exposure.
Expect higher potential returns but also significantly larger drawdowns and leverage decay.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly return by concentrating in high-momentum tech and leveraging strong scanner setups, including 3x ETFs. It accepts substantially higher risk, including amplified drawdowns and the effects of leveraged ETF decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta tech (SMH, CRWD) to zero.
Trim SPY to reduce overall market exposure and free up capital.
Significantly increase allocation to QQQI for its income generation.
Buy GLD for diversification and CVX for its dividend yield.
This plan prioritizes generating steady monthly income and capital preservation.
Expect a low target monthly return around 1%, with minimal drawdowns.
income.read: This income plan focuses on generating steady monthly distributions, targeting around 1% per month. It drastically cuts leverage and high-growth tech, shifting capital to income-focused ETFs and stable dividend payers. Drawdowns are minimized, but growth potential is also limited.
